svg.text: Prefix Sum + Hash Map — Subarray Sum Equals K
Count subarrays with sum k. sum(A[l..r]) = P[r + 1] − P[l] = k.
Equivalent: count pairs (l, r + 1) with P[l] = P[r + 1] − k.
Walk r from 0 to n. Maintain map: prefix value → count of indices with that value.
At each r, add map[P[r] − k] to answer, then increment map[P[r]].
Initialise map with {0: 1} so prefixes equal to k are counted.
• 560. Subarray Sum Equals K — count subarrays summing to k
• 974. Subarray Sums Divisible by K — key on P[i] mod k
• 525. Contiguous Array — map 0→−1, 1→+1, then longest subarray with sum 0
• 1248. Count Number of Nice Subarrays — odd→1, even→0, count subarrays with sum k
• 930. Binary Subarrays With Sum — same shape, binary array
• 523. Continuous Subarray Sum — first repeated remainder under mod k
Recognition: subarray-sum (or count) under a constraint, negatives allowed ⟹ prefix + hash.
